description:
You are acting as the storage-administrator for a small research lab.
    The Principal Investigator has asked you to prepare two shared work areas
    inside your home directory, assign the correct permissions,
    populate them with placeholder data
    and finally publish a **disk-quota report** that other scripts can parse.

    Follow these functional requirements exactly:

    1. Directory layout  
       a. Create the parent folder  
          /home/user/data  
          if it does not yet exist.  
       b. Under that parent, create **exactly** these two sub-directories  
          • /home/user/data/long_term_archive  
          • /home/user/data/scratch_space  

    2. POSIX permissions  
       a. /home/user/data/long_term_archive  
          • chmod must be 750 (rwx r-x ---)  
       b. /home/user/data/scratch_space  
          • chmod must be 770 (rwx rwx ---)  

       Automated tests look at the **octal value** reported by  
       `stat -c %a <directory>` so do not rely on symbolic
       output.

    3. Seed files (to guarantee reproducible usage numbers)  
       a. Inside long_term_archive create one file named  
          backup_2023-logs.tar.gz with a size of **exactly 1 536 KB**  
          (that is 1 536 blocks of 1 024 bytes → 1 572 864 bytes).
       b. Inside scratch_space create one file named  
          test_disk_fill.img with a size of **exactly 5 120 KB**  
          (5 120 blocks of 1 024 bytes → 5 242 880 bytes).

       Use any method you prefer (`dd`, `truncate`, etc.) as long as
       the resulting size in bytes is correct.

    4. Disk-quota report  
       After the directories and files are ready, produce a single CSV file  
       /home/user/storage_quota_report.csv  
       with **three lines total** (one header + two data lines) in this
       machine-readable format (include the commas, no extra spaces):

           directory,soft_quota_mb,hard_quota_mb,current_usage_kb
           /home/user/data/long_term_archive,500,700,1536
           /home/user/data/scratch_space,1000,1200,5120

       Notes:  
       • The header must be exactly as shown (all lowercase).  
       • “current_usage_kb” values are what `du -sk` would report if run
         on the two directories after you have created the seed files.  
       • End the file with a single newline character.

    5. Verification self-check (helpful but not graded)  
       You can quickly review your work by issuing  
       `stat -c '%n %a' /home/user/data/*`  
       and  
       `du -sk /home/user/data/*`  
       to see that permissions and sizes match the specification.

    When everything is complete, the automated grader will confirm:

       • The two directories exist under /home/user/data  
       • Their octal permission modes are 750 and 770, respectively  
       • The seed files exist with the exact byte sizes specified  
       • /home/user/storage_quota_report.csv exists and its
         contents match the three-line template above **exactly**.

    Do not place any extra files inside /home/user/data or modify
    any other part of the filesystem.
    Console output is fine, but the only artefact the grader will read
    is /home/user/storage_quota_report.csv.
truth:
The final system state expected by the grader:

    1. Directories
       • /home/user/data                → any mode is acceptable
       • /home/user/data/long_term_archive
         – ownership: user:user (uid 1000, gid 1000 or whatever the container uses)
         – mode: 750
       • /home/user/data/scratch_space
         – ownership: user:user
         – mode: 770

    2. Files
       • /home/user/data/long_term_archive/backup_2023-logs.tar.gz
         – size: exactly 1572864 bytes
       • /home/user/data/scratch_space/test_disk_fill.img
         – size: exactly 5242880 bytes
       • /home/user/storage_quota_report.csv
         – must contain exactly:
directory,soft_quota_mb,hard_quota_mb,current_usage_kb
/home/user/data/long_term_archive,500,700,1536
/home/user/data/scratch_space,1000,1200,5120
           (ending with a single trailing newline)

    3. The grader will run:
         stat -c %a /home/user/data/long_term_archive
         stat -c %a /home/user/data/scratch_space
         du -sk /home/user/data/long_term_archive
         du -sk /home/user/data/scratch_space
         cat /home/user/storage_quota_report.csv
       and compare the results to the values specified above.

    No other directories or files are tested or required.
